title: Process group management (PR_SET_PDEATHSIG)
description:
Provenance REQ: jn70zc9m0vfsxrwv8smne7kxt583vnwz

PR_SET_PDEATHSIG set on child before exec ensures child receives SIGTERM if noscope dies (SIGKILL, crash, OOM). Process group kill on exit ensures grandchildren are also terminated. Linux-specific, defense-in-depth on top of TTL. Does not apply to mint mode.

Rules:
- NS-024: Process group management in run mode — PR_SET_PDEATHSIG on child; create new process group; kill entire group on exit; prevents orphaned grandchildren retaining credentials

Provenance stats: 0 resolutions, 1 rule

title: Signal handling policy
description:
Provenance REQ: jn764cw67wc4h6d9st0qpe903x83vbgz

The hard part of the tool. What happens when the parent is killed? Revocation must be best-effort with configurable timeout. SIGKILL cannot be caught so rely on TTL expiry as safety net.

Rules:
- NS-003: Revoke on exit guarantee — attempt revocation on child exit regardless of reason; best-effort with timeout+retry; SIGKILL exception relies on TTL
- NS-011: TTL as safety net — ultimate revocation guarantee; if SIGKILL/network-down/retries-exhausted, credential still expires at TTL; never mint without TTL
- NS-014: Revocation must be idempotent — already-revoked/expired treated as success (exit 0)
- NS-026: Signal forwarding policy — SIGTERM/SIGHUP/SIGINT forwarded to child process group; configurable grace period (default 30s); SIGKILL after grace; then revoke; ignore SIGPIPE
- NS-027: Revocation timeout and retry budget — configurable wall-clock budget (default 10s); exponential backoff (base 500ms, max 3 retries); budget=0 disables
- NS-028: Double-signal escalation — second SIGTERM/SIGINT during shutdown: immediately SIGKILL process group, abandon revocation, log, exit; TTL is safety net
- NS-029: Multi-credential revocation on signal — revoke all active credentials in parallel within budget; one failure must not prevent others
- NS-066: Minimum TTL enforcement — minimum 60 seconds; prevents misconfiguration and high-frequency token generation
- NS-067: Maximum TTL enforcement — configurable max (default 12 hours); rejects excessive TTLs; max blast radius for mint mode

Provenance stats: 0 resolutions, 9 rules

title: Token redaction and credential hygiene
description:
Provenance REQ: jn78q255e10n376bzcy350dh6583tq99

Token values must never appear in logs, stderr, or error messages. Use first 8 chars with ellipsis or token_id. Credentials exist only in child process environment and in memory during session.

Rules:
- NS-001: No credential storage — never persist/cache/store to disk or durable medium
- NS-005: Token redaction in output — never in logs/stderr/stdout/errors; use redacted identifiers
- NS-012: No tokens in process arguments — never pass as CLI args (/proc/*/cmdline visible); env vars or FDs only
- NS-019: Memory zeroization on drop — zeroize all in-memory token values; implement Zeroize+ZeroizeOnDrop; no implicit Clone
- NS-020: Core dump prevention — setrlimit(RLIMIT_CORE, 0) at startup before credentials loaded
- NS-058: Debug logging redaction invariant — applies at ALL log levels; no flag/env may disable; redacting newtype for Display+Debug
- NS-059: Redaction format for short and structured tokens — <=16 chars: [redacted:token_id]; JWTs: never use prefix; hash-based ID only

Provenance stats: 2 resolutions, 7 rules

title: Standalone mint and revoke subcommands
description:
Provenance REQ: jn79an6qv3j60bq64c8mekwjkx83tprp

noscope mint outputs scoped token to stdout for caller to deliver (SSH, k8s secret, API call). noscope revoke explicitly revokes a previously minted token. Decouples credential lifecycle from local subprocess management.

Rules:
- NS-060: Mint output envelope — JSON to stdout: token (string), expires_at (ISO 8601), token_id (provider-supplied or UUID), provider, role
- NS-061: Revoke CLI input contract — accept --token-id+--provider or full mint JSON via --from-stdin; token values never as CLI args (NS-012)
- NS-062: Mint mode TTL requirement — --ttl required; no refresh/revocation unless explicit noscope revoke; caller responsibility after mint
- NS-063: Mint multi-provider atomicity — follows NS-006; output is JSON array or nothing on failure; no partial stdout
- NS-064: Redaction exception for mint stdout — NS-005 does not apply to mint stdout (by definition contains token); still applies to stderr/logs
- NS-065: Terminal detection for mint — if stdout isatty, warn to stderr and exit 64 unless --force; tokens in scrollback are a risk

Provenance stats: 3 resolutions, 6 rules

title: CLI: provider discovery and configuration
description:
Provenance REQ: jn72kkrbwj1gk2k10zxsqz13d583vws2

CLI binary: noscope --provider <name> --role <role> --ttl <duration> -- <command>. Provider config at ~/.config/noscope/providers/<name>.toml. Fallback to env vars (NOSCOPE_MINT_CMD etc.) or inline flags (--mint-cmd).

Rules:
- NS-007: Strict config precedence — flags > env vars > config files; no merging across layers; highest-precedence wins entirely
- NS-042: Config follows XDG Base Directory — XDG_CONFIG_HOME/noscope/ default ~/.config/noscope/; providers/<name>.toml, profiles/<name>.toml
- NS-043: Malformed config is hard error — syntactically invalid or missing required fields = exit error; never silently fall through; missing file = layer absent, not error
- NS-044: Provider not found enumerates checked locations — print file path, env var name, flag name checked at each layer; sufficient for user diagnosis
- NS-069: Config file permission enforcement — reject world-readable files (must be 0600 or 0640 on Unix)
- NS-071: Dry-run mode — --dry-run validates config, resolves provider, prints commands with substituted vars; no real minting
- NS-073: Provider validation command — noscope validate --provider <name> verifies mint command exists+executable, config well-formed; no real credentials

Provenance stats: 1 resolution, 7 rules

title: Multiple credentials per invocation
description:
Provenance REQ: jn78wxpmbj1kegw5c8qv05nmzn83tbgd

Real agent tasks need credentials from multiple services. Each provider mints independently with its own env_key. If one fails, revoke all successfully minted and exit error. Concurrent minting for performance.

Rules:
- NS-006: Atomic multi-credential minting — if any provider fails, revoke all minted, exit error; no partial credential sets
- NS-045: Env key uniqueness — reject credential sets with duplicate env_key; validate before minting; identify conflicting providers
- NS-046: Parallel minting timeout — per-provider timeout (default 30s); exceed triggers atomic rollback per NS-006
- NS-047: Atomic rollback follows revocation budget — same timeout/retry policy as signal-triggered revocation (NS-027); log failure with credential ID+TTL
- NS-048: Independent refresh scheduling — each credential has own refresh timer based on its TTL/expires_at; no sync/batching
- NS-049: Single credential expiry preserves child — one expired does not terminate child; log warning; do not re-mint
- NS-050: Bounded parallelism for provider operations — configurable max concurrent (default 8); prevents FD exhaustion/rate limits

Provenance stats: 0 resolutions, 7 rules

title: Refresh failure policy
description:
Provenance REQ: jn77se3zt6bbvvrsw8yakg72e983taj2

If TTL is 30m and refresh fails at 25m, agent still has 5 minutes valid. Killing child on refresh failure is too aggressive. Log, retry, let token expire naturally. TTL is the safety net.

Rules:
- NS-008: Refresh failure preserves child — must not terminate child; retry with exponential backoff; log warning; allow current token until TTL expiry
- NS-025: Refresh limitation documentation — env var injection is point-in-time; warn at startup for rotate mode
- NS-030: Refresh retry parameters — exponential backoff with jitter: base 1s, 2x multiplier, max 4 retries, +/-25% jitter; total retry window <= 50% remaining lifetime
- NS-031: Refresh failure independence — in multi-credential mode, one failure must not affect others
- NS-032: Continuous refresh after failure — single failure window does not permanently disable refresh; keep trying at normal interval

Provenance stats: 1 resolution, 5 rules

title: AgentProcess: spawn, lifecycle, exit code passthrough
description:
Provenance REQ: jn76esa0pkmnsd7s4y5m5ft36s83tkh7

AgentProcess struct: spawn with credential injection, signal forwarding to child, exit code passthrough, revoke-on-exit guarantee. Process must exit with child's exit code, not noscope's.

Rules:
- NS-002: Exit code passthrough — exit with child's exit code, transparent in pipelines
- NS-013: Stdout belongs to child or mint output — run mode: no stdout from noscope; mint mode: only token JSON; all diagnostics to stderr
- NS-021: Strip noscope config from child environment — remove all NOSCOPE_* env vars before spawn
- NS-022: Env var collision is fatal error — if target env_key already set, exit with error; --force to override
- NS-023: Signal-killed child exit code convention — 128+signal_number (POSIX); 127=not found, 126=permission denied
- NS-057: Global wall-clock timeout — --timeout for total lifecycle, kill child + revoke on exceed

Provenance stats: 2 resolutions, 6 rules

title: Provider exit code protocol
description:
Provenance REQ: jn77vzj84ryddct3m131vcjath83td16

Define non-zero exit code protocol for provider commands. Without this, every provider reports errors differently and noscope can't give useful feedback.

Rules:
- NS-010: Provider error exit codes — 0=success, 1=general error, 2=auth failure, 3=role/scope not found, 4=provider unavailable/timeout
- NS-054: noscope exit code range — sysexits.h: 64=usage, 65=mint failure, 66=config not found, 69=unavailable, 70=internal, 77=permission, 78=config error; child exit code used when child ran
- NS-055: Signal-terminated provider handling — exit >128 treated as exit 1, include signal number in stderr
- NS-056: Multi-provider error reporting — report all failures to stderr, exit 65, first failure must not shadow subsequent

Provenance stats: 3 resolutions, 4 rules

title: Provider contract: external command protocol
description:
Provenance REQ: jn7dcw8ce9gxw6v4p9p1k44jex83vgsn

Provider is any CLI that accepts {role}/{ttl} template vars, outputs JSON {token, expires_at}, exits 0 on success. Provider config is TOML with [commands] section for mint/refresh/revoke.

Rules:
- NS-009: Provider output contract — JSON with 'token' (required) and 'expires_at' (optional ISO 8601)
- NS-033: Template variable injection prevention — argv array substitution, never shell; role validated alphanumeric+hyphens+underscores+dots
- NS-034: Missing expires_at computed from requested TTL — now() + requested_ttl with warning
- NS-035: Provider command execution timeout — default 30s, SIGTERM then SIGKILL after 5s, treat as exit 4
- NS-036: Provider stdout size limit — reject output exceeding 1 MiB
- NS-037: TTL format is integer seconds for providers — human durations are CLI concern only
- NS-038: Revoke command receives token via env var — NOSCOPE_TOKEN, NOSCOPE_TOKEN_ID; exit 0 for already-revoked
- NS-039: Refresh command receives token via env var — NOSCOPE_TOKEN, NOSCOPE_TOKEN_ID, NOSCOPE_TTL; same JSON output as mint
- NS-040: Provider stderr handling — capture 4096 bytes on failure, discard on success unless --verbose, redact tokens
- NS-041: Provider capability declaration — supports_refresh, supports_revoke booleans in config
- NS-068: Provider command environment sandboxing — minimal env: PATH, HOME, LANG only

Provenance stats: 2 resolutions, 11 rules

title: Core library: TokenProvider trait + ScopedToken struct
description:
Provenance REQ: jn7e50xjb2j4gp6b5vpjz2qw8x83t1ax

Rust library with TokenProvider trait (async mint/refresh/revoke) and ScopedToken struct (value, role, expires_at, metadata). Foundational primitive all consumers build on.

Rules:
- NS-015: TokenProvider method signatures — async mint(role, ttl), refresh(token), revoke(token); object-safe, Send+Sync
- NS-016: ScopedToken mandatory expiry and zeroizing type — non-optional expires_at, secrecy::SecretString, no Clone without zeroize
- NS-017: Refresh returns new token, does not mutate — caller replaces old token
- NS-018: Dropped futures must not leak credentials — cleanup handler if mint future dropped

Provenance stats: 6 resolutions, 4 rules

title: Implement noscope: scoped credential manager for AI agents
description: Epic covering all 14 provenance requirements for the noscope Rust tool. noscope mints short-lived, scoped credentials for agent processes, manages their lifecycle (refresh, revoke-on-exit, TTL safety net), and supports both local (run mode) and distributed (mint mode) architectures. Provider-agnostic via external command contract.

title: Threat model documentation
description:
Provenance REQ: jn780pzjkgn2dq9geb9mqq7b4h83vcb9

Must distinguish run mode (three safety layers: process groups + revoke-on-exit + TTL) from mint mode (one layer: TTL). Must acknowledge /proc/*/environ risks, credential logging via env vars, fork inheritance to grandchildren, and the SIGKILL gap honestly. Must not claim simplicity equals security.

Rules:
- NS-070: Structured event logging — emit structured events to stderr (JSON-per-line with --log-format json) for: mint/refresh/revoke start/success/fail, child spawn/exit, signal received/forwarded; each event includes timestamp, type, provider, redacted token ID, duration

Provenance stats: 0 resolutions, 1 rule

title: Named profiles
description:
Provenance REQ: jn72g5z02grqpm2adxh30hrpv183t7vt

Profiles at ~/.config/noscope/profiles/<name>.toml. Each is a [[credentials]] array. noscope --profile <name> -- command. Reduces typing for common multi-credential setups. Flat list only -- no extends, no overrides, no merge strategy.

Rules:
- NS-004: Profile flatness constraint — flat lists of (provider, role, ttl) tuples only; no inheritance/extends/overrides/composition
- NS-051: Profile schema — [[credentials]] entries with provider (required), role (required), ttl (required), env_key (optional); unknown top-level fields ignored; unknown credential fields = error; empty array = error
- NS-052: Profile validation before minting — schema+env_key uniqueness+provider existence validated before any minting; all errors reported together; distinct exit code
- NS-053: Profile and CLI flag mutual exclusion — --profile forbids --provider/--role/--ttl; global flags remain valid

Provenance stats: 0 resolutions, 4 rules

title: Provider contract versioning
description:
Provenance REQ: jn73sxs5hdg2j862zpfyqgbz8h83vjbr

Provider config must include contract_version = 1. When mint output format, exit code protocol, or input contracts change, version increments. noscope must support current and previous version for backward compat.

Rules:
- NS-072: Provider contract version — config must include contract_version=1, reject unsupported versions, support current and previous version
